feed.title: WorldDishAtlas
feed.subtitle: The atlas of the world's dishes. Canonical recipes, histories, and where to eat them.

entry.title: Cacio e Pepe
entry.summary: Roman pasta of pecorino, black pepper, and starchy pasta water. Three ingredients, no fat outside the cheese. The cleanest, hardest Roman dish to make at home.

entry.title: Carbonara
entry.summary: Roman pasta of guanciale, eggs, pecorino romano and black pepper. Four ingredients, no cream, served immediately. The most-copied and most-corrupted dish in Italian cooking.

entry.title: Pad Thai
entry.summary: Thai stir-fried rice noodles with tamarind, fish sauce, palm sugar, dried shrimp, egg, tofu, peanuts, and lime. National dish, but invented in the 1930s, not centuries old.

entry.title: Pho Bo
entry.summary: Vietnamese beef noodle soup. Long-simmered beef-bone broth scented with charred ginger, onion, star anise, cloves, cinnamon. Rice noodles, thin-sliced raw beef, fresh herbs at the table.

entry.title: Pizza Margherita
entry.summary: Neapolitan pizza of tomato, mozzarella, basil, olive oil. The three colors of the Italian flag, baked at 480 C for 90 seconds in a wood-fired oven.

entry.title: Shoyu Ramen
entry.summary: Tokyo-style ramen with a soy-based tare, chicken-pork dashi, alkaline wheat noodles, chashu pork, menma, scallion, and ajitama soft egg. The original ramen.

entry.title: Tacos al Pastor
entry.summary: Mexico City pork tacos cooked vertical-spit shawarma-style, marinated in achiote, chiles, and pineapple. Served on small corn tortillas with onion, cilantro, lime, and a slice of charred pineapple.
